### Ferries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_South_Wales&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Ferries")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/85/MV_Charlotte_at_Neutral_Bay_Wharf_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-MV_Charlotte_at_Neutral_Bay_Wharf_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:MV_Charlotte_at_Neutral_Bay_Wharf_(cropped).jpg)

A [First Fleet-class ferry](/wiki/First_Fleet-class_ferry "First Fleet-class ferry") at [Neutral Bay](/wiki/Neutral_Bay "Neutral Bay")

[Transdev Sydney Ferries](/wiki/Transdev_Sydney_Ferries "Transdev Sydney Ferries") operates [Sydney Ferries services](/wiki/Sydney_Ferries "Sydney Ferries") within Sydney Harbour and the [Parramatta River](/wiki/Parramatta_River "Parramatta River"), while [Newcastle Transport](/wiki/Newcastle_Transport "Newcastle Transport") has a ferry service within Newcastle.[[127]](#cite_note-136) All other ferry services are privately operated.[[128]](#cite_note-137)

[Spirit of Tasmania](/wiki/Spirit_of_Tasmania "Spirit of Tasmania") ran a commercial ferry service between Sydney and [Devonport, Tasmania](/wiki/Devonport,_Tasmania "Devonport, Tasmania"). This service was terminated in 2006.[[129]](#cite_note-138)

Private boat services operated between South Australia, Victoria and New South Wales along the Murray and Darling Rivers but these only exist now as the occasional tourist paddle-wheeler service.[[130]](#cite_note-139)

## National parks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_South_Wales&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: National parks")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d2/Three_Sisters_-Blue_Mountains%2C_New_South_Wales%2C_Australia-20July2012.jpg/250px-Three_Sisters_-Blue_Mountains%2C_New_South_Wales%2C_Australia-20July2012.jpg)](/wiki/File:Three_Sisters_-Blue_Mountains,_New_South_Wales,_Australia-20July2012.jpg)

*Three Sisters* rock formation in the [Blue Mountains National Park](/wiki/Blue_Mountains_National_Park "Blue Mountains National Park")

See also: [Protected areas of New South Wales](/wiki/Protected_areas_of_New_South_Wales "Protected areas of New South Wales")

New South Wales has more than 780 national parks and reserves covering more than 8% of the state.[[131]](#cite_note-140) These parks range from rainforests, waterfalls, rugged bush to marine wonderlands and outback deserts, including [World Heritage](/wiki/World_Heritage_Site "World Heritage Site") sites.[[132]](#cite_note-141)

The [Royal National Park](/wiki/Royal_National_Park "Royal National Park") on the southern outskirts of Sydney became Australia's first national park when proclaimed on 26 April 1879. Originally named simply 'National Park' until 1955, this park was the second national park to be established in the world after [Yellowstone National Park](/wiki/Yellowstone_National_Park "Yellowstone National Park") in the U.S. [Kosciuszko National Park](/wiki/Kosciuszko_National_Park "Kosciuszko National Park") is the largest park in state encompassing New South Wales' alpine region.[[133]](#cite_note-142)

The National Parks Association was formed in 1957 to create a system of national parks all over New South Wales which led to the formation of the [National Parks and Wildlife Service](/wiki/National_Parks_and_Wildlife_Service_(New_South_Wales) "National Parks and Wildlife Service (New South Wales)") in 1967.[[134]](#cite_note-143) This government agency is responsible for developing and maintaining the parks and reserve system, and conserving natural and cultural heritage, in the state of New South Wales. These parks preserve special habitats, plants and wildlife, such as the [Wollemi National Park](/wiki/Wollemi_National_Park "Wollemi National Park") where the [Wollemi Pine](/wiki/Wollemia "Wollemia") grows and areas sacred to Australian Aboriginals such as [Mutawintji National Park](/wiki/Mutawintji_National_Park "Mutawintji National Park") in western New South Wales.

## Sport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_South_Wales&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Sport")]

Main article: [Sport in New South Wales](/wiki/Sport_in_New_South_Wales "Sport in New South Wales")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/State_of_Origin_Game_II_2018.jpg/250px-State_of_Origin_Game_II_2018.jpg)](/wiki/File:State_of_Origin_Game_II_2018.jpg)

[Stadium Australia](/wiki/Stadium_Australia "Stadium Australia")

The most popular sports by participation in the state are [soccer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") and tennis.[[135]](#cite_note-:0-144) The [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"), which is based in Sydney, is the dominant professional spectator sport.[[135]](#cite_note-:0-144) In rugby league, the state is represented by the [New South Wales Blues](/wiki/New_South_Wales_rugby_league_team "New South Wales rugby league team") in the [State of Origin series](/wiki/State_of_Origin_series "State of Origin series"). The state hosts 10 of the 17 [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") teams: the [Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs](/wiki/Canterbury-Bankstown_Bulldogs "Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs"), [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks"), [Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles](/wiki/Manly-Warringah_Sea_Eagles "Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles"), [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights"), [Parramatta Eels](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels"), [Penrith Panthers](/wiki/Penrith_Panthers "Penrith Panthers"), [St George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St_George_Illawarra_Dragons "St George Illawarra Dragons"), [South Sydney Rabbitohs](/wiki/South_Sydney_Rabbitohs "South Sydney Rabbitohs"), [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters"), and [Wests Tigers](/wiki/Wests_Tigers "Wests Tigers"). Other rugby league competitions in the state include the [NSW Cup](/wiki/NSW_Cup "NSW Cup"), the [Sydney A-Grade](/w/index.php?title=List_of_NSWRL_Conference_Clubs_in_Sydney_by_Competition&action=edit&redlink=1 "List of NSWRL Conference Clubs in Sydney by Competition (page does not exist)"), and regional competitions administered by the NSWRL's [Country Rugby League](/wiki/Country_Rugby_League "Country Rugby League") division.

The main summer sport is cricket, and the [Sydney Cricket Ground](/wiki/Sydney_Cricket_Ground "Sydney Cricket Ground") hosts the 'New Year' cricket [Test match](/wiki/Test_cricket "Test cricket") in January each year. The [NSW Blues](/wiki/New_South_Wales_cricket_team "New South Wales cricket team") play in the [One-Day Cup](/wiki/Australian_domestic_limited-overs_cricket_tournament "Australian domestic limited-overs cricket tournament") and [Sheffield Shield](/wiki/Sheffield_Shield "Sheffield Shield") competitions. [Sydney Sixers](/wiki/Sydney_Sixers "Sydney Sixers") and [Sydney Thunder](/wiki/Sydney_Thunder "Sydney Thunder") both play in the [Big Bash League](/wiki/Big_Bash_League "Big Bash League").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3f/DJR_Team_Penske_2019_Bathurst_1000_Practice_4.jpg/250px-DJR_Team_Penske_2019_Bathurst_1000_Practice_4.jpg)](/wiki/File:DJR_Team_Penske_2019_Bathurst_1000_Practice_4.jpg)

The [Bathurst 1000](/wiki/Bathurst_1000 "Bathurst 1000"), held at [Mount Panorama Circuit](/wiki/Mount_Panorama_Circuit "Mount Panorama Circuit") in [Bathurst](/wiki/Bathurst,_New_South_Wales "Bathurst, New South Wales")

The state is represented in the [Australian Football League](/wiki/Australian_Football_League "Australian Football League") by the [Sydney Swans](/wiki/Sydney_Swans "Sydney Swans"), and the [Greater Western Sydney Giants](/wiki/Greater_Western_Sydney_Giants "Greater Western Sydney Giants") who entered the competition in 2012.

The state is represented by five teams in [soccer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football")'s [A-League](/wiki/A-League "A-League"): [Sydney FC](/wiki/Sydney_FC "Sydney FC"), [Western Sydney Wanderers](/wiki/Western_Sydney_Wanderers_FC "Western Sydney Wanderers FC") ([2014 Asian champions](/wiki/2014_AFC_Champions_League "2014 AFC Champions League")), [Central Coast Mariners](/wiki/Central_Coast_Mariners_FC "Central Coast Mariners FC"), [Newcastle Jets](/wiki/Newcastle_Jets_FC "Newcastle Jets FC") and [Macarthur FC](/wiki/Macarthur_FC "Macarthur FC").

Other teams in major national competitions include the [Sydney Kings](/wiki/Sydney_Kings "Sydney Kings") and [Illawarra Hawks](/wiki/Hawks_(NBL) "Hawks (NBL)") in the [National Basketball League](/wiki/National_Basketball_League_(Australia) "National Basketball League (Australia)"), [Sydney Uni Flames](/wiki/Sydney_Uni_Flames "Sydney Uni Flames") in the [Women's National Basketball League](/wiki/Women%27s_National_Basketball_League "Women's National Basketball League"), [New South Wales Waratahs](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Waratahs "New South Wales Waratahs") in [Super Rugby](/wiki/Super_Rugby "Super Rugby") and [New South Wales Swifts](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Swifts "New South Wales Swifts") in [Super Netball](/wiki/Super_Netball "Super Netball").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/49/Sydney_Cricket_Ground_%2824509044622%29.jpg/250px-Sydney_Cricket_Ground_%2824509044622%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sydney_Cricket_Ground_(24509044622).jpg)

The [Sydney Cricket Ground](/wiki/Sydney_Cricket_Ground "Sydney Cricket Ground")

Sydney was the host of the [1938 British Empire Games](/wiki/1938_British_Empire_Games "1938 British Empire Games") and [2000 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2000_Summer_Olympics "2000 Summer Olympics"). The [Stadium Australia](/wiki/Stadium_Australia "Stadium Australia") hosts major events including the [NRL Grand Final](/wiki/NRL_Grand_Final "NRL Grand Final"), [State of Origin](/wiki/State_of_Origin_series "State of Origin series"), rugby union and soccer internationals.

The annual [Sydney to Hobart Yacht Race](/wiki/Sydney_to_Hobart_Yacht_Race "Sydney to Hobart Yacht Race") begins in Sydney Harbour on Boxing Day. [Bathurst](/wiki/Bathurst,_New_South_Wales "Bathurst, New South Wales") hosts the annual [Bathurst 1000](/wiki/Bathurst_1000 "Bathurst 1000") as part of the [Supercars Championship](/wiki/Supercars_Championship "Supercars Championship") at [Mount Panorama Circuit](/wiki/Mount_Panorama_Circuit "Mount Panorama Circuit").

The equine sports of [campdrafting](/wiki/Campdrafting "Campdrafting") and [polocrosse](/wiki/Polocrosse "Polocrosse") were developed in New South Wales and competitions are now held across Australia. Polocrosse is now played in many overseas countries.

Other professional teams include:

* [Baseball](/wiki/Australian_Baseball_League "Australian Baseball League"): [Sydney Blue Sox](/wiki/Sydney_Blue_Sox "Sydney Blue Sox")
* [Ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey"): [Newcastle Northstars](/wiki/Newcastle_Northstars "Newcastle Northstars"), [Sydney Bears](/wiki/Sydney_Bears "Sydney Bears"), [Sydney Ice Dogs](/wiki/Sydney_Ice_Dogs "Sydney Ice Dogs")
* [Motor racing](/wiki/Supercars_Championship "Supercars Championship"): [Brad Jones Racing](/wiki/Brad_Jones_Racing "Brad Jones Racing"), [Team Sydney](/wiki/Tekno_Autosports "Tekno Autosports")

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_South_Wales&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: Culture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/Palace_Hotel%2C_Broken_Hill_NSW.jpg/250px-Palace_Hotel%2C_Broken_Hill_NSW.jpg)](/wiki/File:Palace_Hotel,_Broken_Hill_NSW.jpg)

The Palace Hotel in [Broken Hill](/wiki/Broken_Hill "Broken Hill"), the only town in Australia to be listed on the [National Heritage List](/wiki/Australian_National_Heritage_List "Australian National Heritage List")[[136]](#cite_note-145)[[137]](#cite_note-146)

As Australia's most populous state, New South Wales is home to several cultural institutions of importance to the nation. In music, New South Wales is home to the [Sydney Symphony Orchestra](/wiki/Sydney_Symphony_Orchestra "Sydney Symphony Orchestra"), Australia's busiest and largest orchestra. Australia's largest opera company, [Opera Australia](/wiki/Opera_Australia "Opera Australia"), is headquartered in Sydney. Both of these organisations perform a subscription series at the Sydney Opera House. Other major musical bodies include the [Australian Chamber Orchestra](/wiki/Australian_Chamber_Orchestra "Australian Chamber Orchestra"). Sydney is host to the [Australian Ballet](/wiki/Australian_Ballet "Australian Ballet") for its Sydney season (the ballet is headquartered in Melbourne). Apart from the [Sydney Opera House](/wiki/Sydney_Opera_House "Sydney Opera House"), major musical performance venues include the [City Recital Hall](/wiki/City_Recital_Hall "City Recital Hall") and the [Sydney Town Hall](/wiki/Sydney_Town_Hall "Sydney Town Hall").

New South Wales is home to several major museums and art galleries, including the [Australian Museum](/wiki/Australian_Museum "Australian Museum"), the [Powerhouse Museum](/wiki/Powerhouse_Museum "Powerhouse Museum"), the [Museum of Sydney](/wiki/Museum_of_Sydney "Museum of Sydney"), the [Art Gallery of New South Wales](/wiki/Art_Gallery_of_New_South_Wales "Art Gallery of New South Wales") and the [Museum of Contemporary Art](/wiki/Museum_of_Contemporary_Art,_Sydney "Museum of Contemporary Art, Sydney").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Art_Gallery_of_New_South_Wales_viewed_from_The_Domain.jpg/250px-Art_Gallery_of_New_South_Wales_viewed_from_The_Domain.jpg)](/wiki/File:Art_Gallery_of_New_South_Wales_viewed_from_The_Domain.jpg)

The [Art Gallery of New South Wales](/wiki/Art_Gallery_of_New_South_Wales "Art Gallery of New South Wales")

Sydney is home to five Arts teaching organisations, which have all produced world-famous students: The National Art School, The College of Fine Arts, the National Institute of Dramatic Art [(NIDA)](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Dramatic_Art "National Institute of Dramatic Art"), the Australian Film, Television & Radio School and the [Conservatorium of Music](/wiki/Sydney_Conservatorium_of_Music "Sydney Conservatorium of Music") (now part of the University of Sydney).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

New South Wales is the setting and shooting location of many Australian films, including *[Mad Max 2](/wiki/Mad_Max_2 "Mad Max 2")*, which was shot near the mining town of Broken Hill. The state has also attracted international productions, both as a setting, such as in *[Mission: Impossible 2](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_2 "Mission: Impossible 2")*, and as a stand-in for other locations, as seen in *[The Matrix](/wiki/The_Matrix_(franchise) "The Matrix (franchise)")* franchise, *[The Great Gatsby](/wiki/The_Great_Gatsby_(2013_film) "The Great Gatsby (2013 film)")* and *[Unbroken](/wiki/Unbroken_(film) "Unbroken (film)")*.[[138]](#cite_note-147)[[139]](#cite_note-148) [20th Century Fox](/wiki/20th_Century_Fox "20th Century Fox") operates [Fox Studios Australia](/wiki/Fox_Studios_Australia "Fox Studios Australia") in Sydney. [Screen NSW](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Film_and_Television_Office "New South Wales Film and Television Office"), which controls the state film industry, generates approximately $100 million into the New South Wales economy each year.[[140]](#cite_note-149)

## Sister states

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_South_Wales&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Sister states")]

New South Wales in recent history has pursued bilateral partnerships with other federated states/provinces and metropolises through establishing a network of [sister state](/wiki/Twin_towns_and_sister_cities "Twin towns and sister cities") relationships.
The state currently has 7 sister states:[[141]](#cite_note-150)

* [Guangdong](/wiki/Guangdong "Guangdong"), China (since 1979)
* [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"), Japan (since 1984)
* [Ehime](/wiki/Ehime_Prefecture "Ehime Prefecture"), Japan (since 1999)[[142]](#cite_note-151)
* [North Rhine-Westphalia](/wiki/North_Rhine-Westphalia "North Rhine-Westphalia"), Germany (since 1989)
* [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul"), South Korea (since 1991)
* [Jakarta](/wiki/Jakarta "Jakarta"), Indonesia (since 1994)
* [California](/wiki/California "California"), United States (since 1997)
